content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Jeanne Pritchard                                    Date : June 22, 1976

                                                         76-06-226

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION - Ken Goshorn

Subject: Letter of April 29 from Supervisor Jim Bates


     On or about May 28 I spoke with Mr. Bates on the
telephone. We discussed the matter in question as per
a previous conversation at the opinion request meeting.
We agreed that it did not appear that the ball clubs
were making any reportable gift, but that he would
report it as a gift from the Stadium Authority. Mr.
Bates seemed satisfied with the informal advice I
gave him. He did not request any written response.


KG/g

SUPERVISOR JIM BATES

COUNTY OF SAN DIEGO


April 29, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street Mall
Sacramento, California    95814

Dear Sir:

As a member of the San Diego Board of Supervisors, I am in receipt
of season tickets to the San Diego Padre and Charger games, as the
result of use of a box reserved for San Diego Stadium Authority
members which includes the City Council and Board of Supervisors.

This box has never been leased to the Padres or Chargers.  These
tickets have a combined value of approximately $892.00, which I am
not sure should be claimed in my financial disclosure forms; but I
am sure that a value should be placed on it.

Could you please advise me if the San Diego City Council and the
Board of Supervisors should declare this amount as a gift, or how
should it be handled?

A ruling on this matter would be appreciated.

Sincerely,


JIM BATES, Vice Chairman
Board of Supervisors

JB/kb

sections.question: As a member of the San Diego Board of Supervisors, I am in receipt of season tickets to the San Diego Padre and Charger games, as the result of use of a box reserved for San Diego Stadium Authority members which includes the City Council and Board of Supervisors. This box has never been leased to the Padres or Chargers. These tickets have a combined value of approximately $892.00, which I am not sure should be claimed in my financial disclosure forms; but I am sure that a value should be placed on it. Could you please advise me if the San Diego City Council and the Board of Supervisors should declare this amount as a gift, or how should it be handled?
sections.conclusion: We discussed the matter in question as per a previous conversation at the opinion request meeting. We agreed that it did not appear that the ball clubs were making any reportable gift, but that he would report it as a gift from the Stadium Authority. Mr. Bates seemed satisfied with the informal advice I gave him.
sections.question_synthetic: Should Supervisor Jim Bates report season tickets valued at approximately $892.00 received through a San Diego Stadium Authority box as a gift in his financial disclosure forms?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised informally that the ball clubs were not making a reportable gift, but recommended reporting the tickets as a gift from the Stadium Authority instead.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an informal advice letter documented in a memorandum format rather than a formal response letter. The FPPC response was given verbally on May 28, 1976, and no written response was requested. The original question letter is dated April 29, 1976.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As a member of the San Diego Board of Supervisors, I am in receipt of season tickets to the San Diego Padre and Charger games, as the result of use of a box reserved for San Diego Stadium Authority members which includes the City Council and Board of Supervisors. This box has never been leased to the Padres or Chargers. These tickets have a combined value of approximately $892.00, which I am not sure should be claimed in my financial disclosure forms; but I am sure that a value should be placed on it. Could you please advise me if the San Diego City Council and the Board of Supervisors should declare this amount as a gift, or how should it be handled?

CONCLUSION: We discussed the matter in question as per a previous conversation at the opinion request meeting. We agreed that it did not appear that the ball clubs were making any reportable gift, but that he would report it as a gift from the Stadium Authority. Mr. Bates seemed satisfied with the informal advice I gave him.

We discussed the matter in question as per a previous conversation at the opinion request meeting. We agreed that it did not appear that the ball clubs were making any reportable gift, but that he would report it as a gift from the Stadium Authority. Mr. Bates seemed satisfied with the informal advice I gave him. He did not request any written response. KG/g SUPERVISOR JIM BATES COUNTY OF SAN DIEGO April 29, 1976 Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 "K" Street Mall Sacramento, California 95814 Dear Sir: As a member of the San Diego Board of Supervisors, I am in receipt of season tickets to the San Diego Padre and Charger games, as the result of use of a box reserved for San Diego Stadium Authority members which includes the City Council and Board of Supervisors. This box has never been leased to the Padres or Chargers. These tickets have a combined value of approximately $892.00, which I am not sure should be claimed in my financial disclosure forms; but I am sure that a value should be placed on it. Could you please advise me if the San Diego City Council and the Board of Supervisors should declare this amount as a gift, or how should it be handled? A ruling on this matter would be appreciated. Sincerely, JIM BATES, Vice Chairman Board of Supervisors JB/kb
embedding.summary: Supervisor Jim Bates inquired whether season tickets valued at $892.00 from a San Diego Stadium Authority box should be reported as a gift. FPPC staff advised informally that the tickets should be reported as a gift from the Stadium Authority rather than from the ball clubs.
